title: Wireless Zone Security Monitoring — Unknown Clients on OT SSID

compliance.assurance_rationale: Uplifted from contributing to partial: UC provides meaningful monitoring evidence for SR 1.1.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that IEC 62443 SR 1.1 (Human user identification and authentication) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.15.53: Wireless Zone Security Monitoring — Unknown Clients on OT SSID.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.15.53' running on meraki:airmarshal or cisco:wlc, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

spl:
index=wireless sourcetype="meraki:association" OR sourcetype="cisco:wlc" ssid="OT-PROD-*" earliest=-24h
| iplocation src_ip
| lookup ot_wireless_allowlist.csv client_mac OUTPUT asset_class
| where asset_class!="instrumentation"
| stats earliest(_time) as first_seen, latest(_time) as last_seen by client_mac, ssid, ap_name
| sort - last_seen
description: Lists Wi-Fi associations to OT WLANs from unknown MAC vendors or non-instrumentation OUI prefixes.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for IEC 62443 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes wireless and sourcetypes cisco:wlc, meraki:association. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) WPA3-Enterprise with cert; (2) dynamic PSK per device class; (3) block consumer OUIs; (4) RF site survey after changes. Indexes required: `index=wireless`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=cisco:wlc`, `sourcetype=meraki:association`; Lookups: `ot_wireless_allowlist`, `ot_wireless_allowlist.csv`.
visualization: Table, Map, Time chart. Key fields: `ap_name`, `client_mac`, `first_seen`, `last_seen`, `ssid`.
knownFalsePositives: Zone communications during authorized engineering, vendor maintenance, or planned commissioning.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: WLAN controller syslog.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `meraki:airmarshal` or `cisco:wlc`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) WPA3-Enterprise with cert; (2) dynamic PSK per device class; (3) block consumer OUIs; (4) RF site survey after changes.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=wireless sourcetype="meraki:association" OR sourcetype="cisco:wlc" ssid="OT-PROD-*" earliest=-24h
| iplocation src_ip
| lookup ot_wireless_allowlist.csv client_mac OUTPUT asset_class
| where asset_class!="instrumentation"
| stats earliest(_time) as first_seen, latest(_time) as last_seen by client_mac, ssid, ap_name
| sort - last_seen
```

Understanding this SPL

**Wireless Zone Security Monitoring — Unknown Clients on OT SSID (IEC 62443-3-2 / Wireless zone security monitoring)** — Lists Wi-Fi associations to OT WLANs from unknown MAC vendors or non-instrumentation OUI prefixes.

Documented **Data sources**: `meraki:airmarshal` or `cisco:wlc`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): WLAN controller syslog. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: wireless; **sourcetype**: meraki:association, cisco:wlc. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=wireless, sourcetype="meraki:association", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.

• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Filters the current rows with `where asset_class!="instrumentation"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` aggregates events by **client_mac, ssid, ap_name**, producing one row per unique combination for trend analysis and threshold evaluation.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.
Output fields: ap_name, client_mac, first_seen, last_seen, ssid.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the OT firewall and the approved zone-to-zone matrix for that window. Spot-check a few source events, reconcile counts, and verify field extractions, thresholds, and index permissions for the people who need this evidence.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table, Map, Time chart.
grandmaExplanation: We lists Wi-Fi associations to OT WLANs from unknown MAC vendors or non-instrumentation OUI prefixes. That helps protect factories, plants, and the gear that runs them from mistakes and bad actors the standards call out.
